title: Basic Wire Welding Overview and Safety Requirements
summary: This section introduces the fundamental wire welding processes available on the Vulcan OmniPro 220, including flux-cored, MIG, and aluminum welding. It outlines the protective gear required before welding and emphasizes the importance of practicing on scrap material before working on actual projects.
key_facts: Flux-cored wire welding welds mild steel and stainless steel without shielding gas.
MIG welding uses solid wire and shielding gas to weld mild steel and stainless steel.
MIG welding can weld thinner workpieces than flux-cored welding can.
Aluminum welding requires an optional Spool Gun sold separately, using aluminum wire and shielding gas.
Good welding takes a degree of skill and experience.
Practice welds on scrap are recommended before welding a different thickness of material.
Practice welds on scrap are recommended before welding a different type of material.
Practice welds on scrap are recommended before welding a different type of connection.
Practice welds on scrap are recommended whenever using a different process.
Minimum shade number 10 full face shield or welding mask is required.
Light from the arc can cause permanent damage to the eyes and skin.
Keep welding tip clear of grounded objects whenever unit is plugged in and turned on.
warnings: TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY: Protective gear must be worn when using the Welder; minimum shade number 10 full face shield (or welding mask), ear protection, welding gloves, sleeves and apron, NIOSH-approved respirator, and fire resistant work clothes without pockets should be worn when welding.
Light from the arc can cause permanent damage to the eyes and skin.
TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY, FIRE AND BURNS: Keep welding tip clear of grounded objects whenever unit is plugged in and turned on.
Read the ENTIRE IMPORTANT SAFETY INFORMATION section at the beginning of this manual including all text under subheadings therein before welding.
